ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., active, completed, on hold) */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the project */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project manager */,
  "priority_level" INTEGER /* Priority level of the project */,
  "stakeholders" TEXT /* Key stakeholders involved in the project */,
  "industry_standards_compliance" TEXT /* Compliance with industry standards */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projects_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirements" (
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each requirement */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the requirement belongs to */,
  "requirement_text" TEXT /* Text description of the requirement */,
  "requirement_type" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement type */,
  "level_specific" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is level-specific */,
  "l1" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is L1 level */,
  "l2" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is L2 level */,
  "l3" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is L3 level */,
  "l4" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is L4 level */,
  "l5" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is L5 level */,
  "not_level_specific" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement is not level-specific */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the requirement */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the requirement was created */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the requirement */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date the requirement was last updated */,
  "status" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement status */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the requirement */,
  "estimated_completion_date" TEXT /* Estimated completion date of the requirement */,
  "actual_completion_date" TEXT /* Actual completion date of the requirement */,
  "estimated_effort_hours" REAL /* Estimated effort required in hours */,
  "actual_effort_hours" REAL /* Actual effort spent in hours */,
  "risk_level" INTEGER /* Risk level of the requirement */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the requirement on the project */,
  "related_requirements" TEXT /* IDs of related requirements */,
  "estimated_cost" REAL /* Estimated cost of fulfilling the requirement */,
  "actual_cost" REAL /* Actual cost incurred */,
  "approver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the requirement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirements_last_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirements_approver_id FOREIGN KEY ("approver_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirements_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirements_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirements_requirement_type FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_type") REFERENCES requirement_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirements_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES requirement_status ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., software engineer, project manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "avatar_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each requirement type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the requirement type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the requirement type */,
  "is_customizable" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the requirement type can be customized */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the type */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the type was created */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the type */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date the type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each requirement status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the requirement status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the requirement status */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the status is the default status */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the status */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the status was created */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the status */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date the status was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement the comment is related to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the comment has been resolved */,
  "related_documents" TEXT /* IDs of related documents */,
  "is_private" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the comment is private */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_comments_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_documents" (
  "document_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement the document is related to */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of the document (e.g., design, test plan) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the document */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the document in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the document was uploaded */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the document */,
  "is_confidential" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the document is confidential */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the document */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("document_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_documents_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_documents_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY ("uploaded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement that depends on another requirement */,
  "dependent_requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement being depended on */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of the dependency (e.g., blocks, requires, is blocked by) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dependency */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the dependency */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the dependency on the project */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the dependency */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date the dependency was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_dependencies_dependent_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_dependencies_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_dependencies_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "version_text" TEXT /* Text description of the requirement in this version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the requirement in this version (e.g., draft, approved) */,
  "change_summary" TEXT /* Summary of the changes made in this version */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the version */,
  "approved_date" TEXT /* Date the version was approved */,
  "is_current_version" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_versions_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_versions_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_approvals" (
  "approval_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each approval */,
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement being approved */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the requirement */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date the requirement was approved */,
  "approval_status" TEXT /* Status of the approval (e.g., approved, rejected) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments on the approval */,
  "approval_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the approval or rejection */,
  "related_documents" TEXT /* IDs of related documents */,
  "is_final_approval" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this is the final approval */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("approval_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_approvals_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_approvals_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requirement_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the requirement the metric is related to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., completion rate, lead time) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric */,
  "is_key_metric" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the metric is a key performance indicator (KPI) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requirement_metrics_requirement_id FOREIGN KEY ("requirement_id") REFERENCES requirements ("requirement_id")
)
